compiled_state.author: Iso Tester
compiled_state.description:
## Summary
Epic dark-acme-lumen (#98) added the kind-keyed `state['attestations']` projection in `reducer/_processors.py:process_signature`, but did NOT bump `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` in `reducer/_cache.py` (still 3). A build WITHOUT the attestations projection (e.g. an older global `pipx` install) writes a version-3 `.cache.json` that lacks `attestations`; a newer build sharing the same `.tickets-tracker` then serves that stale cache (matching dir_hash) and reports NO attestations.

## Impact
The plan-review CLAIM gate (`require_plan_review_for_claim`) verifies STRICTLY from `state['attestations']['plan-review']` (signing.verify_signature(kind=...)). A stale cache makes a genuinely-certified attestation read as 'unsigned', so `rebar claim` is BLOCKED on a validly-reviewed ticket. Observed live on b744 (the legacy `signature` mirror + `verify-signature` certify fine; the kind-keyed map is absent until the cache is deleted and the ticket re-reduced). Affects any mixed-build environment (the dogfood checkout's local build + the global pipx build / MCP server / hooks).

## Reproduction Steps
1. Earn a plan-review attestation on a ticket with a build that has the attestations projection.
2. Have an older build (no attestations projection, same `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION=3`) read the ticket (writes a stale cache).
3. `rebar claim <id>` -> 'no certified plan-review attestation (signature: unsigned)' despite `verify-signature` certifying.

Expected: a projection change invalidates prior caches (claim succeeds on a certified ticket).
Actual: stale cache served; claim wrongly blocked.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_REDUCER_CACHE_VERSION` is bumped whenever the reduced-state projection changes (bump it for the attestations addition), so caches written by a build with different projection logic are never served.
- [ ] A test asserts that a ticket carrying a signed plan-review SIGNATURE event reduces to a populated `state['attestations']['plan-review']` even when a pre-projection `.cache.json` is present.
- [ ] Workaround documented for mixed-build checkouts (delete `.cache.json` / single-build env).

## Notes
Discovered while implementing the code-review epic (b744). Workaround in use: delete the ticket's `.cache.json` immediately before `rebar claim`. NOT fixed here (out of scope for the code-review capability).
compiled_state.title: Reducer cache not invalidated by kind-keyed attestations projection (stale .cache.json hides state['attestations'])
